data.body:
PASS 1 review — G6 (approach soundness). Two grounded findings + affirmations.

**[G6] (a) `criteria=[G6]` · `location`: AC line "grounding/harness.py:281 typo fixed; the except BaseException no longer swallows KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit"**

- [ ] Reconsider whether narrowing harness.py:281 to stop catching `KeyboardInterrupt`/`SystemExit` is correct here — this is the `_worker_entry` *child-subprocess* trampoline whose documented invariant is fail-open: ANY error in the worker (incl. a `KeyboardInterrupt`/`SystemExit` raised inside the child) must ship `("err", ...)` so the PARENT maps it to an abstain and the host never crashes.

  evidence:
  • `src/rebar/grounding/harness.py:271-287` — `_worker_entry` runs in the worker process; the `except BaseException` ships every error back over the pipe.
  • `src/rebar/grounding/harness.py:314-335` — `run_in_worker` docstring: "Fail-open mappings (each → an abstain_reason, NEVER a raise) … the worker raises → other". The whole boundary is "never a host crash".
  • Narrowing to `except Exception` (the obvious reading of "no longer swallows KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit") would let `SystemExit`/`KeyboardInterrupt` raised in the child escape `conn.send(("err", ...))`, so the child dies without shipping a payload — changing the fail-open mechanism into a different (signal-death) path.
  • The floor's "KeyboardInterrupt/SystemExit are never swallowed" rule (`src/rebar/_logging.py:35-39`) targets in-PROCESS broad catches that would eat the operator's Ctrl-C; a child-process trampoline is the recognised exception — cf. the established precedent `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile.py:1155` `except BaseException … # noqa: BLE001 — must re-raise after recording`.

  suggested_fix: Reframe AC#2 to: fix the `BLE0001`→`BLE001` typo and KEEP `except BaseException` with a justified `# noqa: BLE001` reason string (fail-open worker trampoline; the parent maps the shipped error to an abstain). If any narrowing IS intended, the plan must say so explicitly and state how the fail-open invariant is preserved for `SystemExit`/`KeyboardInterrupt` raised inside the child — otherwise the as-written AC coaches the implementer to break the documented mechanism.

**[G6] (b) `criteria=[G6]` · `location`: What bullet "Remove these packages from the BLE001/T201 allowlist as they become conformant" + AC line "These packages removed from the allowlist"**

- [ ] Reconcile the "remove `_engine_support` from the T201 allowlist" goal with the established design that classifies `_engine_support` T201 as a LEGITIMATE-PRINT surface that STAYS — fully removing the entry will either fail `make lint` (AC#4) or wrongly migrate machine-contract stdout.

  evidence:
  • `pyproject.toml:211-215` — `"src/rebar/_engine_support/*" = ["BLE001", "T201"]` with the inline note: "_engine_support T201 is the data-CLI stdout contract (reads_cli/validate/next_batch)".
  • `pyproject.toml:192-201` — the allowlist split: `_engine_support` is named under "legitimate-print surfaces … the data-CLI surfaces keep a scoped ignore", NOT under "transitional — diagnostics that MIGRATE".
  • `src/rebar/_logging.py:20-23` — stdout `print(json.dumps(...))` is a machine contract the reconciler `json.loads`; "stdout data prints stay." `_engine_support/reads_cli.py:90,92,219,221,…` are exactly these.
  • These files ALSO carry free-form stderr diagnostics (e.g. `reads_cli.py:48,86,116` `print(..., file=sys.stderr)`) — so the package is a mix, not a clean migrate-everything target.

  suggested_fix: State that `_engine_support` keeps a SCOPED T201 ignore (stdout data-CLI surface) rather than being removed outright — i.e. migrate only the free-form stderr diagnostics where appropriate, KEEP the `print(json.dumps(...))` stdout, and drop only BLE001 (and T201 only if every print is genuinely removable, which the data contract says it is not). Make AC#3/AC#4 distinguish "stderr diagnostics migrate" from "data-contract stdout stays + keeps a justified T201 ignore", matching the close-out's own classification.

**Affirmations (criteria PASSED for G6):**
• MECHANISM for the BLE0001→BLE001 typo fix itself is correct & well-targeted: the extra `0` does make the noqa inert (verified — `harness.py:281` is the lone `BLE0001` in the tree; every other site is `BLE001`), so fixing it is real and necessary.
• APPROACH SELECTION is defensible for the grounding/ BLE001 work: bringing broad-excepts to the observability floor via inline log-or-justified-noqa matches the codebase-wide strangler pattern (`_logging.py:25-39`) and the per-package allowlist-shrink already used by sibling sweeps (8d01/ceaa/fd72) — no structurally-better alternative is being missed; a shared swallow-helper is explicitly rejected by the floor (`_logging.py:33`), so the plan correctly avoids it.
• POSITIVE rationale is present and adequate (fail-open/observability floor, named-logger migration, shrinking allowlist) — the MINOR rationale-absence check passes.
